""" import network import socket import asyncio import machine import time import json import config_manager AP_SSID = "ScaleBrain" AP_PASSWORD = "" AP_IP = "192.168.4.1" STA_TIMEOUT_S = 30 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # LED helpers # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- _led = machine.Pin("LED", machine.Pin.OUT) async def _led_pulse(on_ms: int, off_ms: int, stop_event: asyncio.Event = None): while True: _led.on() await asyncio.sleep_ms(on_ms) _led.off() await asyncio.sleep_ms(off_ms) if stop_event and stop_event.is_set(): break def led_solid(): _led.on() def led_off(): _led.off() # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # STA (client) mode # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _sync_ntp(log=None): try: import ntptime ntptime.settime() if log: t = time.localtime() log(f"NTP sync OK: {t[0]:04d}-{t[1]:02d}-{t[2]:02d} " f"{t[3]:02d}:{t[4]:02d}:{t[5]:02d} UTC") except Exception as e: if log: log(f"NTP sync failed (non-fatal): {e}") def connect_sta(ssid: str, password: str) -> bool: def _slog(msg): print(f"[WiFi] {msg}") try: with open("boot_log.txt", "a") as f: f.write(f"+{time.ticks_ms():>9}ms [STA] {msg}\n") except Exception: pass wlan = network.WLAN(network.STA_IF) wlan.active(True) network.hostname("scalebrain") if wlan.isconnected() and wlan.config("essid") == ssid: _slog(f"Already connected to {ssid}") set_ip(wlan.ifconfig()[0]) _sync_ntp(_slog) led_solid() return True MAX_ATTEMPTS = 5 for attempt in range(1, MAX_ATTEMPTS + 1): _slog(f"Attempt {attempt}/{MAX_ATTEMPTS}: connecting to '{ssid}' …") wlan.connect(ssid, password) attempt_deadline = time.ticks_add(time.ticks_ms(), STA_TIMEOUT_S * 1000) blink = True status_interval = 0 while not wlan.isconnected(): if time.ticks_diff(attempt_deadline, time.ticks_ms()) <= 0: break try: st = wlan.status() except Exception: st = "?" if isinstance(st, int) and st < 0: _slog(f"CYW43 failure (status={st}) — will retry.") wlan.disconnect() time.sleep_ms(1000) break _led.value(blink) blink = not blink time.sleep_ms(200) status_interval += 1 if status_interval % 25 == 0: _slog(f"Waiting … {status_interval * 200}ms status={st}") if wlan.isconnected(): ip = wlan.ifconfig()[0] set_ip(ip) _slog(f"Connected on attempt {attempt}. IP: {ip}") _sync_ntp(_slog) led_solid() return True try: st = wlan.status() except Exception: st = "?" _slog(f"All {MAX_ATTEMPTS} attempts failed. status={st}") wlan.active(False) return False _cached_ip = "" def set_ip(ip: str): global _cached_ip _cached_ip = ip def get_ip() -> str: return _cached_ip # Getting Started

Scale Brain is a precision weighing application running on a Raspberry Pi Pico 2W with an HX711 load cell amplifier. It provides real-time weight readings, gas cylinder monitoring, and integration with the KegLevel Brain app for keg management.

First-Time Setup

  1. Power on the Scale Brain Pico. If no Wi-Fi is configured, it will start in Access Point (AP) mode and broadcast a network named ScaleBrain-XXXX.
  2. Connect to the ScaleBrain-XXXX network from your phone or computer.
  3. A captive portal will appear (or navigate to 192.168.4.1). Select your home Wi-Fi network and enter the password.
  4. The Pico will reboot, connect to your Wi-Fi, and begin serving the Scale Brain web app at its assigned IP address.
  5. Calibrate the scale (see Calibration) before taking measurements.
↑ Back to contents

Scale Screen

The main screen shows the current weight and provides quick access to tare, hold, unit selection, keg integration, and gas cylinder monitoring.

When two scales are configured (see Multi-Scale Configuration), each scale is displayed as a separate card with its own weight display, STABLE/HOLD badges, TARE/HOLD buttons, unit selector, Kegs section, and Gas Cylinders section.

Weight Display

The large number at the top shows the current weight. The unit label (kg, lb, or lb / oz) appears directly below, along with status badges:

  • STABLE — appears when the weight reading has remained consistent for at least 2 seconds. This indicates the reading is reliable.
  • HOLD — appears when Hold mode is active (see below).

Weights are rounded to 10 grams (0.01 kg) for consistency. Display precision varies by unit: kg and lb show 2 decimal places; lb / oz shows whole pounds and whole ounces.

Tare

Tap TARE to zero the scale. Place any container or platform on the scale first, then tap TARE so subsequent readings reflect only the contents. Tare can also be used from the Calibration settings tab.

Hold

Tap HOLD to enable Hold mode. When enabled, the display will capture the first stable reading after an object is placed on the scale and freeze that value until Hold is toggled off. This is useful for recording a weight without drift affecting the display.

If the scale is already stable when Hold is enabled, it will wait for a new object to be placed (a change in weight) before capturing and freezing.

Unit Selection

Three unit buttons appear below the action bar:

Button Display Format Example
kg Kilograms, 2 decimal places 3.91 kg
lb Pounds (decimal), 2 decimal places 8.62 lb
lb / oz Pounds and whole ounces 8 lb 10 oz

The selected unit is remembered across sessions and also determines the unit shown during calibration.

Kegs (KegLevel Integration)

If you have a KegLevel Brain on the same network, Scale Brain can pull in your keg list and push tare/full weights back to KegLevel.

  1. Go to SETTINGS → SYSTEM and enter the KegLevel IP Address (e.g., 192.168.68.50). Tap SAVE.
  2. Back on the SCALE screen, the Kegs section will show your keg list in a dropdown.
  3. Select a keg. The current tare and full weights stored in KegLevel are displayed.
  4. Place the empty keg on the scale. Tap SET TARE WEIGHT to send the current scale reading to KegLevel as that keg's tare weight.
  5. Fill the keg and place it on the scale. Tap SET FULL WEIGHT to send the current reading as the full weight.
  6. Tap REFRESH at any time to reload the keg list from KegLevel.
The KegLevel Pico must be powered on and reachable on the network. If the IP address is not set or the device is unreachable, an error message will appear in the Kegs section.

Gas Cylinders

Gas Cylinders let you monitor CO2 or N2 tank levels by tracking how much gas remains based on weight.

  1. Tap NEW to create a cylinder. Enter a name (e.g., "20 lb CO2 Tank").
  2. Enter the Gas Capacity — the rated gas weight (e.g., 20 lb for a 20 lb CO2 tank). This is the weight of the gas only, not the tank.
  3. Enter the Full Weight — the total weight on the scale when the tank is full (tank + regulator + hoses + gas). You can type a value or tap SET FROM SCALE to capture the current reading.
  4. Tap SAVE.

The setup weight (tank + regulator + hoses, without gas) is calculated automatically as Full Weight minus Gas Capacity.

With a cylinder selected, place the tank on the scale. The readout shows:

  • Remaining — estimated weight of gas remaining.
  • Percentage — how full the tank is relative to its rated capacity.
  • A fill bar provides a visual indicator.

When you swap in a new tank, tap SET FULL to capture the new full weight from the scale. The gas capacity stays fixed, and the setup weight is recalculated automatically.

Use the Report to KegLevel dropdown to send CO2 or N2 percentage data to the KegLevel Brain app, where it appears in the header alongside the temperature reading.

Settings — System

The System tab shows network information and configuration options.

Scale Configuration (Multi-Scale)

Scale Brain supports up to two independent scales, each connected to its own HX711 load cell amplifier on separate GPIO pins.

Scale SCK Pin DT Pin
Scale 1 GP27 GP28
Scale 2 GP21 GP22

To enable a second scale:

  1. Wire the second HX711 module to GP21 (SCK) and GP22 (DT), plus 3V3 and GND.
  2. Go to SETTINGS → SYSTEM and change Number of Scales from 1 to 2.
  3. The Pico will reboot to initialize the second HX711.
  4. After reboot, two scale cards appear on the Scale screen. Each has independent tare, hold, and calibration.
Each scale must be calibrated independently. In the Calibration tab, use the Select Scale dropdown to choose which scale to calibrate.

Wi-Fi

Displays the currently connected SSID, the Pico's IP Address, and the Mode (Station or AP).

KegLevel Brain Integration

Enter the IP address of your KegLevel Brain Pico (e.g., 192.168.68.50). Do not include http:// — it is added automatically. Tap SAVE to store the address.

Once saved, the Kegs section on the Scale screen will attempt to connect to KegLevel and load your keg list.

Network vs. Standalone Mode

Scale Brain can operate in two modes:

Mode Description
Network Connects to your home WiFi router. Any device on the same network can access Scale Brain. OTA firmware updates, KegLevel integration, and all features are available.
Standalone The Pico creates its own WiFi network (ScaleBrain). Connect your phone or laptop directly. No internet access, firmware updates, or KegLevel integration.

To switch modes, use the Enable Network Mode or Enable Standalone Mode buttons at the bottom of the System tab. The button for the mode you are currently in is disabled. Switching reboots the Pico into the captive portal where you confirm the new mode.

When in Standalone mode, the header title shows a bordered style as a visual reminder. The Updates tab also displays a notice that OTA updates are unavailable.

Settings — Calibration

Calibration tells the scale how to convert raw sensor readings into real-world weight units. You will need a known reference weight (e.g., a dumbbell, bag of sugar, or calibration weight).

Calibration Steps

If you have two scales configured, first select the scale you want to calibrate from the Select Scale dropdown at the top of the Calibration tab. The live weight display and TARE button will operate on the selected scale.

  1. Clear the scale. Remove everything from the platform.
  2. Tap TARE to zero the scale.
  3. Place your known weight on the scale. Wait for the STABLE badge to appear on the live weight display.
  4. Enter the known weight in the input field. The field adapts to the currently selected unit:
    • kg — enter kilograms with up to 3 decimal places (e.g., 3.910)
    • lb — enter pounds with up to 3 decimal places (e.g., 8.620)
    • lb / oz — enter whole pounds and whole ounces in the two fields
  5. Tap CALIBRATE. The scale factor is computed and saved immediately.
If Hold mode is active, calibration uses the held (frozen) raw value instead of the current live reading. This is helpful to avoid drift between placing the weight and tapping CALIBRATE.

When to Recalibrate

  • After moving the scale to a different surface.
  • If readings seem inaccurate compared to known references.
  • After a firmware update that resets configuration (rare).

Settings — Updates

The Updates tab lets you check for and install firmware updates over the air (OTA).

Checking for Updates

Tap CHECK FOR UPDATES. The app contacts GitHub to fetch the latest firmware manifest. The current and latest available versions are displayed. If a newer version is available, the INSTALL UPDATE button appears.

What's New

When an update is available, a What's New section appears showing the release notes for each version newer than the one currently installed. This helps you decide whether to update.

Installing an Update

Tap INSTALL UPDATE to begin the OTA process. Files are downloaded from GitHub and uploaded to the Pico one at a time. A progress bar shows status. Files that have not changed (matching hash) are skipped automatically.

After installation, the Pico reboots. Wait a few seconds and then reload the page.

Changelog

A complete changelog is shown at the bottom of the Updates tab, listing all past releases with their dates and notes.

Reload

Tap RELOAD to force a full cache-clearing page reload. This is useful after an OTA update to ensure the browser loads the latest version of all files.

Resetting Wi-Fi Configuration

If you need to connect the Pico to a different Wi-Fi network:

  1. Locate the reset button on the Pico (GP15).
  2. Press and hold the button for 4 seconds. The LED will double-blink while held, then turn solid when the threshold is reached.
  3. Release the button. The Pico will clear its saved Wi-Fi credentials and reboot into AP (provisioning) mode.
  4. Connect to the ScaleBrain-XXXX network and re-enter your Wi-Fi details through the captive portal.
The reset button is ignored during active scale operations. Calibration settings and gas cylinders are preserved across a Wi-Fi reset.

Troubleshooting

Scale readings are wildly fluctuating

  • Ensure the scale is on a stable, level surface. Soft or uneven surfaces amplify noise.
  • Check that the HX711 wiring is secure. Loose connections on E+/E-/A+/A- cause erratic readings.
  • Keep signal wires (DT and SCK) as short as possible. Long runs pick up electrical noise.
  • Try recalibrating after ensuring stable readings.

Web page won't load (but Pico responds to ping)

  • A missing firmware file can crash the HTTP server. Re-deploy all firmware files using deploy.ps1.
  • Check error.log on the Pico for ImportError messages.

Kegs section shows an error

  • Verify the KegLevel IP Address is correct in SETTINGS → SYSTEM.
  • Ensure the KegLevel Pico is powered on and connected to the same network.
  • Tap REFRESH in the Kegs section to retry.

OTA update fails

  • Ensure the Pico has a stable internet connection.
  • Large files are transferred in chunks. If a transfer times out, try again — unchanged files are skipped automatically.
  • After a failed update, tap RELOAD and try CHECK FOR UPDATES again.

LED is blinking in a loop

This indicates the firmware is crash-looping. Connect via mpremote and check boot_log.txt or error.log for details. Re-deploying all firmware files usually resolves the issue.

Second scale shows — (no reading)

  • Verify that the second HX711 is wired to GP21 (SCK) and GP22 (DT).
  • Confirm Number of Scales is set to 2 in SETTINGS → SYSTEM. Changing this requires a reboot.
  • Check that the second HX711 has power (3V3 and GND connected) and the load cell is properly wired to E+/E-/A+/A-.

Pico is in AP mode unexpectedly

The Pico enters AP (provisioning) mode when it cannot connect to a saved Wi-Fi network. This can happen if the router is offline, the password changed, or a Wi-Fi reset was triggered. Connect to the ScaleBrain-XXXX AP and reconfigure.
